```markdown
# Implementation Prompt - Milestone 20 Assistant Mode Deployment Profiles

You are IBM Bob working in the RepoQuest repository.

Implement Milestone 20: **Assistant Mode Deployment Profiles**.

Milestones 15-19 should already be complete: optional AI assistant mode exists, provider validation/evals are in place, local model support is available, Docker/async assistant service exists, and the UI clearly separates deterministic analysis from AI-assisted review.

Your job is to make deployment modes explicit, documented, testable, and hard to confuse.

## Product Goal

RepoQuest must support multiple deployment profiles without compromising the default deterministic app.

Users and judges should immediately understand which mode is running:

- Deterministic Code Assistant.
- Local AI Assistant.
- Local Model Assistant.
- Hosted AI Assistant.

The default app must remain safe, no-secret, deterministic, and Streamlit Community Cloud compatible.

## Required Baseline

Assume these are available:

- Deterministic Code Assistant Workbench.
- Optional AI assistant mode.
- Mock provider.
- Claude/cloud provider.
- Local model provider.
- Async assistant service.
- Docker Compose setup.
- Assistant validation/evals.
- Clean UI shell with assistant state.
- `docs/assistant_mode.md` already exists or can be updated.

Do not change the product architecture unless required to expose/deploy the profiles cleanly.

## Deployment Profiles

### 1. Deterministic Code Assistant Profile

This is the default and safest profile.

Requirements:

- No secrets.
- No required runtime AI.
- Optional AI disabled.
- Streamlit Community Cloud compatible.
- Bundled demo repo works.
- ZIP analysis works.
- Application graph excludes tests by default.
- Deterministic tasks, workflows, docs, and test intelligence work.
- No assistant service required.
- No local model required.
- README and UI clearly label this as deterministic/no-secret mode.

Example:

```bash
make run
```

or:

```bash
docker compose up --build repoquest
```

### 2. Local AI Assistant Profile

This profile is for development and experimentation.

Requirements:

- Optional local secrets.
- Mock provider by default in tests.
- Real provider only when explicitly enabled.
- Docker Compose profile can run Streamlit and assistant service as separate services.
- App talks to assistant service through `REPOQUEST_ASSISTANT_SERVICE_URL`.
- Can target a cloud provider or local model provider.
- UI shows AI-enabled mode label.
- AI failures do not break deterministic analysis.

Mock example:

```bash
REPOQUEST_AI_ENABLED=true \
REPOQUEST_ASSISTANT_SERVICE_URL=http://assistant:8765 \
REPOQUEST_ASSISTANT_SERVICE_PROVIDER=mock \
docker compose --profile assistant up --build
```

Claude example:

```bash
REPOQUEST_AI_ENABLED=true \
REPOQUEST_ASSISTANT_SERVICE_URL=http://assistant:8765 \
CLAUDE_API_KEY=your_key_here \
CLAUDE_MODEL=claude-sonnet-4-20250514 \
docker compose --profile assistant up --build
```

### 3. Local Model Profile

This profile supports private/local model experimentation.

Requirements:

- No cloud AI key required.
- Local model server runs on host or another container.
- Assistant service calls local model endpoint.
- Recommended first target: OpenAI-compatible local endpoint.
- Example providers:
  - Ollama with OpenAI-compatible endpoint.
  - LM Studio.
  - llama.cpp server.
- Still uses bounded context packs.
- Still uses validation and trust gates.
- Mock-mode tests are required.
- Live local model calls are manual/dev-only.
- Local model unavailable errors appear as assistant errors only.

Example:

```bash
REPOQUEST_AI_ENABLED=true \
REPOQUEST_ASSISTANT_SERVICE_URL=http://assistant:8765 \
REPOQUEST_ASSISTANT_PROVIDER=local \
REPOQUEST_LOCAL_MODEL_BASE_URL=http://host.docker.internal:11434/v1 \
REPOQUEST_LOCAL_MODEL_NAME=llama3.1 \
docker compose --profile assistant up --build
```

### 4. Hosted AI Assistant Profile

This profile is for hosted AI-assisted demos or deployments.

Requirements:

- Requires Streamlit secrets or server-side secret mechanism.
- May use separately hosted assistant service.
- Deterministic Streamlit deployment must still work without service.
- UI shows clear AI-enabled label.
- Includes privacy, cost, and provider notes.
- Preserves deterministic fallback.
- Passes assistant validation/eval tests before demo.
- Does not expose provider keys client-side.

Streamlit secrets example:

```toml
REPOQUEST_AI_ENABLED = "true"
CLAUDE_API_KEY = "your_key_here"
CLAUDE_MODEL = "claude-sonnet-4-20250514"
```

## Documentation Tasks

Update public documentation so deployment profile differences are obvious.

Update or create:

- `README.md`
- `docs/assistant_mode.md`
- `infra/docker/README.md`
- `.env.example`
- Any Streamlit/deployment docs that mention AI mode.

Documentation must include:

- Deployment profile table.
- Deterministic/default profile instructions.
- Local AI assistant setup.
- Local model setup.
- Docker Compose examples.
- Streamlit Cloud secrets instructions.
- Privacy notes.
- Cost notes for hosted/cloud providers.
- Troubleshooting for:
  - Missing keys.
  - Assistant disabled state.
  - Rate limits.
  - Schema failures.
  - Invalid citations.
  - Validation failures.
  - Local model connection failures.
  - Slow local model responses.
  - Assistant service unavailable.
- Explanation of deterministic workflows vs AI-assisted workflows.

## UI Requirements

The app should clearly show:

- Current mode/profile when possible.
- AI disabled vs enabled.
- Provider type:
  - disabled
  - mock
  - Claude/cloud
  - local model
  - assistant service
- Model name when available.
- Service URL/status when configured.
- Friendly error if assistant service or local model is unavailable.
- Deterministic fallback remains available.

Do not show raw secrets.

Do not expose API keys.

## Configuration Requirements

Support or document these variables:

```bash
REPOQUEST_AI_ENABLED=false
REPOQUEST_ASSISTANT_SERVICE_URL=
REPOQUEST_ASSISTANT_SERVICE_TIMEOUT_SECONDS=45
REPOQUEST_ASSISTANT_SERVICE_PROVIDER=
CLAUDE_API_KEY=
CLAUDE_MODEL=claude-sonnet-4-20250514
REPOQUEST_ASSISTANT_PROVIDER=
REPOQUEST_LOCAL_MODEL_BASE_URL=
REPOQUEST_LOCAL_MODEL_NAME=
```

Defaults should keep deterministic mode working.

## Docker Requirements

Docker Compose should support:

- Deterministic app only.
- App plus assistant service in mock mode.
- App plus assistant service using Claude/cloud provider.
- App plus assistant service using local model provider.

The assistant service should expose:

- `GET /health`
- `POST /jobs`
- `GET /jobs/{job_id}`

Mock assistant service must work without network access.

## Tests To Add Or Update

Add or update tests/checks so these pass:

- Deterministic profile runs with no secrets.
- Missing assistant secrets produce friendly disabled state.
- Mock provider works without network access.
- Hosted profile docs explain what data may be sent to provider.
- Assistant profile can be disabled without code changes.
- Docker assistant service can run in mock mode without network access.
- Local model profile can be configured without cloud key.
- Local model unavailable errors are displayed as assistant errors.
- Local model unavailable errors do not break deterministic analysis.
- Profile/config parser identifies deterministic, mock, cloud, service, and local model modes.
- Documentation contains examples for deterministic, mock service, Claude service, and local model service.

Do not require live Docker, Claude, or local model calls in unit tests unless they are clearly marked manual.

## Acceptance Criteria

Milestone 20 is complete when:

- Public docs clearly explain which profile is being demoed.
- The default app remains safe, deterministic, and no-secret.
- AI assistant mode is optional, testable, and clearly labeled.
- Docker/async assistant service setup is documented.
- Local model setup is documented.
- Hosted AI mode is documented with privacy and cost notes.
- Deterministic fallback is preserved in every profile.
- Missing secrets, local model failures, service failures, and validation failures never break deterministic analysis.

## Out Of Scope

Do not make AI required for Streamlit Community Cloud.

Do not commit secrets.

Do not add authentication or user accounts.

Do not add a database.

Do not run live provider calls in CI.

Do not remove deterministic profile support.
```

          "name": "read_file",
          "response": {
            "output": "# AI Assistant Mode\n\n## Overview\n\nRepoQuest includes an **optional AI assistant** powered by Claude that can provide additional insights beyond deterministic static analysis.\n\n**Important:** The AI assistant is:\n- **Disabled by default**\n- **Optional** - all core RepoQuest features work without it\n- **Manual-only** - never runs automatically\n- **Bounded** - only receives capped context packs, not entire repos\n- **Safe** - validates responses to prevent execution claims\n\n## Configuration\n\n### Environment Variables\n\nCreate a `.env` file (not tracked in git):\n\n```bash\ncp .env.example .env\n\n# Enable AI assistant (default: false)\nREPOQUEST_AI_ENABLED=true\n\n# Claude API key (required if enabled)\nCLAUDE_API_KEY=your_api_key_here\n\n# Claude model (optional, default: claude-sonnet-4-20250514)\nCLAUDE_MODEL=claude-sonnet-4-20250514\n\n# Optional async assistant service URL.\n# Use this when Streamlit should submit assistant jobs to a separate service.\nREPOQUEST_ASSISTANT_SERVICE_URL=\nREPOQUEST_ASSISTANT_SERVICE_TIMEOUT_SECONDS=45\n```\n\n### Streamlit Secrets (Cloud Deployment)\n\nFor Streamlit Community Cloud, add secrets in the dashboard:\n\n```toml\nREPOQUEST_AI_ENABLED = \"true\"\nCLAUDE_API_KEY = \"your_api_key_here\"\nCLAUDE_MODEL = \"claude-sonnet-4-20250514\"\n```\n\nIf `REPOQUEST_AI_ENABLED=true` is set while no API key or assistant service is available, RepoQuest keeps deterministic analysis enabled and shows a \"no API key configured\" assistant status.\n\n### Docker Compose Assistant Service\n\nRepoQuest can also run optional AI/model work in a separate service. This keeps\nthe Streamlit UI process focused on deterministic analysis and makes assistant\ncalls asynchronous at the service boundary.\n\nDeterministic app only:\n\n```bash\ndocker compose up --build repoquest\n```\n\nApp plus assistant service in mock mode:\n\n```bash\nREPOQUEST_AI_ENABLED=true \\\nREPOQUEST_ASSISTANT_SERVICE_URL=http://assistant:8765 \\\nREPOQUEST_ASSISTANT_SERVICE_PROVIDER=mock \\\ndocker compose --profile assistant up --build\n```\n\nApp plus assistant service using Claude:\n\n```bash\nREPOQUEST_AI_ENABLED=true \\\nREPOQUEST_ASSISTANT_SERVICE_URL=http://assistant:8765 \\\nCLAUDE_API_KEY=your_api_key_here \\\ndocker compose --profile assistant up --build\n```\n\nThe assistant service exposes:\n\n- `GET /health` for readiness checks.\n- `POST /jobs` to enqueue an assistant request.\n- `GET /jobs/{job_id}` to poll queued/running/completed status.\n\nThe service stores jobs in memory only. Restarting the service clears pending\nassistant jobs, while deterministic RepoQuest analysis remains available.\n\n## Features\n\nWhen enabled, AI assistant buttons appear in these sections:\n\n### 1. Overview Tab\n- **Button:** \"Get AI Insights on Overview\"\n- **Context:** Project type, frameworks, entry points, key folders\n- **Output:** High-level analysis and recommendations\n\n### 2. Architecture Map Tab\n- **Button:** \"Ask AI about this file\" (after selecting a graph node)\n- **Context:** Selected file metadata, imports, routes, tests, and snippets\n- **Output:** Node-specific explanation and follow-up suggestions\n\n### 3. Reading Path Tab\n- **Button:** \"Ask AI to explain...\" (per reading path file)\n- **Context:** File role, capped source preview, and detected routes where relevant\n- **Output:** File explanation, risks, and improvement ideas\n\n### 4. Components Tab\n- **Button:** Component-specific assistant action\n- **Context:** Component role, connections, detected items\n- **Output:** Component-specific insights and improvement ideas\n\n### 5. Tests Tab\n- **Button:** \"Get Test Recommendations\"\n- **Context:** Test inventory, coverage gaps, quality signals\n- **Output:** Prioritized test suggestions\n\n### 6. Work Plans Tab\n- **Button:** \"Refine Work Plan\"\n- **Context:** Generated tasks, workflows, milestones\n- **Output:** Enhanced task descriptions and priorities\n\n### 7. Documentation and Export Tabs\n- **Button:** \"Improve Documentation\"\n- **Context:** Existing docs, detected gaps\n- **Output:** Documentation enhancement suggestions\n\n## Safety Features\n\n### Response Validation\n\nAll AI responses are validated to ensure:\n\n1. **Non-empty responses** - Rejects blank outputs\n2. **Valid file citations** - Only cites files that exist in the scanned repo\n3. **No execution claims** - Rejects responses claiming tests were run\n4. **No execution instructions** - Rejects responses instructing code execution\n\n### Context Limits\n\nAI requests are bounded:\n\n- **Max snippet size:** 800 characters per file\n- **Max files:** Limited by section (typically 5-10)\n- **Safe files only:** Excludes `.env`, secrets, binaries\n- **Static analysis warning:** Every request includes a disclaimer\n\n### Example Validation\n\nRejected:\n```\n\"I ran the tests and they all passed.\"\n```\n\nAccepted:\n```\n\"Based on static analysis of backend/routes/trips.py,\nthe route handlers appear to lack input validation.\"\n```\n\n## Architecture\n\n### Provider Pattern\n\n```python\nclass AssistantProvider(Protocol):\n  def generate(request: AssistantRequest) -> AssistantResponse:\n   ...\n```\n\n**Implementations:**\n- `DisabledAssistantProvider` - Returns \"disabled\"status\n- `MockAssistantProvider` - Returns deterministic test responses\n- `ClaudeAssistantProvider` - Calls Claude API via stdlib urllib\n- `AssistantServiceProvider` - Submits jobs to the optional async assistant service\n\n### Context Builders\n\nEach section has a dedicated context builder:\n\n- `build_overview_context()` - Project summary\n- `build_file_context()` - Single file analysis\n- `build_component_context()` - Component card analysis\n- `build_test_context()` - Test intelligence\n- `build_workflow_context()` - Work plan refinement\n- `build_documentation_context()` - Doc enhancement\n\n### Data Flow\n\n```\nUser clicks AI button\n -> Context builder creates AssistantRequest\n -> Provider generates AssistantResponse directly or submits a service job\n -> Optional service queues/runs the model call asynchronously\n -> Validator checks response\n -> UI displays result or validation error\n```\n\n## Usage Guidelines\n\n### When to Use AI Assistant\n\n **Good use cases:**\n- Getting high-level project insights\n- Understanding complex component relationships\n- Prioritizing test coverage gaps\n- Refining work plan descriptions\n- Improving documentation clarity\n\n **Not suitable for:**\n- Replacing deterministic analysis\n- Executing code or tests\n- Making production decisions\n- Security audits\n- Compliance validation\n\n### Best Practices\n\n1. **Review AI outputs** - Always validate suggestions against actual code\n2. **Use deterministic first** - Generate the quest before using AI\n3. **Cite evidence** - Check that AI citations reference real files\n4. **Stay bounded** - AI only sees capped snippets, not full files\n5. **Manual only** - AI never runs automatically during quest generation\n\n## Cost Considerations\n\n- **API calls:** Each AI button click = 1 Claude API call\n- **Token usage:** Typically 500-1500 tokens per request\n- **Pricing:** See Anthropic pricing for current rates\n- **Free tier:** Deterministic RepoQuest is always free\n\n## Troubleshooting\n\n### AI Assistant Not Available\n\n**Symptom:** Sidebar shows \"AI Assistant is disabled\"\n\n**Solutions:**\n1. Set `REPOQUEST_AI_ENABLED=true` in `.env`\n2. Restart the Streamlit app\n3. Check sidebar status panel\n\n### API Key Not Configured\n\n**Symptom:** Sidebar shows \"AI Assistant enabled but no API key configured\"\n\n**Solutions:**\n1. Set `CLAUDE_API_KEY` in `.env` or Streamlit secrets\n2. Verify key is valid\n3. Restart the app\n\n### Assistant Service Unavailable\n\n**Symptom:** An AI button returns \"Assistant service network error\"\n\n**Solutions:**\n1. Confirm the service is running: `docker compose --profile assistant ps`\n2. Check that the app has `REPOQUEST_ASSISTANT_SERVICE_URL=http://assistant:8765`\n3. Try mock mode first with `REPOQUEST_ASSISTANT_SERVICE_PROVIDER=mock`\n4. Check `GET http://localhost:8765/health` from the host\n\n### Validation Errors\n\n**Symptom:** Warning message after AI response\n\n**Common causes:**\n- AI cited non-existent files -> Check file paths\n- AI claimed test execution -> Regenerate with different phrasing\n- Empty response -> Try again or check API status\n\n## Development\n\n### Adding New AI Features\n\n1. Create context builder in `repoquest/assistant_context.py`\n2. Add button in relevant tab in `app/streamlit_app.py`\n3. Use `run_assistant()` helper function\n4. Display result from `st.session_state[\"assistant_outputs\"]`\n\n### Testing\n\n```bash\n# Run assistant tests\npytest tests/test_assistant.py -v\n\n# Test with mock provider\nREPOQUEST_AI_ENABLED=true pytest tests/test_assistant.py\n```\n\n### Mock Provider for Testing\n\n```python\nfrom repoquest.assistant_provider import MockAssistantProvider\n\nprovider = MockAssistantProvider(\n  mock_response=\"Test response\",\n  mock_citations=[AssistantCitation(file_path=\"test.py\")]\n)\n```\n\n## Comparison: Deterministic vs AI\n\n| Feature | Deterministic | AI Assistant |\n|---------|--------------|--------------|\n| **Always available** | Yes | Requires API key |\n| **Cost** | Free | API costs |\n| **Speed** | Instant | Usually a few seconds |\n| **Reproducible** | Yes | May vary |\n| **Evidence-based** | Always | Validated citations |\n| **Offline** | Yes | Requires network |\n| **Privacy** | Local only | Warning: Sends snippets |\n\n## Privacy & Data\n\n### What Gets Sent to Claude\n\n- Capped code snippets (max 800 chars each)\n- File paths and roles\n- Project type and framework names\n- Detected routes and components\n- **Never sent:** Full files, secrets, binaries, credentials\n\n### Network And Certificates\n\nThe Claude provider uses the Python standard library HTTP client. When available, it uses the local `certifi` certificate bundle to avoid common macOS certificate-chain errors. Network or certificate failures are displayed as assistant errors and do not interrupt deterministic RepoQuest output.\n\nIn Docker assistant mode, the Claude call happens inside the `assistant` service. The Streamlit app receives only the assistant response/job status from the service, then still applies RepoQuest response validation before display.\n\n### Data Retention\n\n- RepoQuest does not store AI responses permanently\n- Responses live in Streamlit session state only\n- Claude API retention: See Anthropic privacy policy\n\n## Future Enhancements\n\nPotential future features (not yet implemented):\n\n- Streaming responses for long outputs\n- Multi-turn conversations\n- Custom system prompts\n- Alternative LLM providers\n- Response caching\n- Persistent job storage beyond the current in-memory queue\n\n## Support\n\nFor issues or questions:\n\n1. Check this documentation\n2. Review validation messages\n3. Test with mock provider\n4. Check Anthropic API status\n5. Open GitHub issue with details\n\n---\n\n**Remember:** AI assistant is optional. RepoQuest's deterministic analysis is the foundation.\n"
          }
        }
      }
    ],

1. **Four deployment profiles:**
   - Deterministic Code Assistant (default, no AI)
   - Local AI Assistant (with mock/Claude)
   - Local Model Profile (Ollama, LM Studio, etc.)
   - Hosted AI Assistant (Streamlit Cloud with secrets)

2. **Key requirements:**
   - Update config.py with deployment profile detection
   - Update documentation (README, assistant_mode.md, docker README, .env.example)
   - Update UI to show current profile/mode
   - Add Docker Compose profiles
   - Add tests for profile detection
   - Ensure deterministic mode remains default and safe

3. **Current state:**
   - config.py exists with basic constants
   - docs/assistant_mode.md exists with good AI documentation
   - Need to check docker-compose.yml, .env.example, README.md

Let me start by creating a comprehensive todo list and then work through the implementation systematically.

This tool is designed for step-by-step task tracking, allowing you to confirm completion of each step before updating, update multiple task statuses at once (e.g., mark one as completed and start the next), and dynamically add new todos discovered during long or complex tasks.\n\n**Checklist Format:**\n- Use a single-level markdown checklist (no nesting or subtasks).\n- List todos in the intended execution order.\n- Status options:\n\t - [ ] Task description (pending)\n\t - [x] Task description (completed)\n\t - [-] Task description (in progress)\n\n**Status Rules:**\n- [ ] = pending (not started)\n- [x] = completed (fully finished, no unresolved issues)\n- [-] = in_progress (currently being worked on)\n\n**Core Principles:**\n- Before updating, always confirm which todos have been completed since the last update.\n- You may update multiple statuses in a single update (e.g., mark the previous as completed and the next as in progress).\n- When a new actionable item is discovered during a long or complex task, add it to the todo list immediately.\n- Do not remove any unfinished todos unless explicitly instructed.\n- Always retain all unfinished tasks, updating their status as needed.\n- Only mark a task as completed when it is fully accomplished (no partials, no unresolved dependencies).\n- If a task is blocked, keep it as in_progress and add a new todo describing what needs to be resolved.\n- Remove tasks only if they are no longer relevant or if the user requests deletion.\n\n**Usage Example:**\n<update_todo_list>\n<todos>\n[x] Analyze requirements\n[x] Design architecture\n[-] Implement core logic\n[ ] Write tests\n[ ] Update documentation\n</todos>\n</update_todo_list>\n\n*After completing \"Implement core logic\" and starting \"Write tests\":*\n<update_todo_list>\n<todos>\n[x] Analyze requirements\n[x] Design architecture\n[x] Implement core logic\n[-] Write tests\n[ ] Update documentation\n[ ] Add performance benchmarks\n</todos>\n</update_todo_list>\n\n**When to Use:**\n- The task is complicated or involves multiple steps or requires ongoing tracking.\n- You need to update the status of several todos at once.\n- New actionable items are discovered during task execution.\n- The user requests a todo list or provides multiple tasks.\n- The task is complex and benefits from clear, stepwise progress tracking.\n\n**When NOT to Use:**\n- There is only a single, trivial task.\n- The task can be completed in one or two simple steps.\n- The request is purely conversational or informational.\n\n**Task Management Guidelines:**\n- Mark task as completed immediately after all work of the current task is done.\n- Start the next task by marking it as in_progress.\n- Add new todos as soon as they are identified.\n- Use clear, descriptive task names.\n",
    "renderOutputAsMarkdown": true
  }
]

time\nimport urllib.request\nimport urllib.error\nfrom pathlib import Path\nfrom typing import Protocol\n\nfrom repoquest.assistant_models import (\n  AssistantRequest,\n  AssistantResponse,\n  AssistantCitation,\n  AssistantJobStatus,\n  CodeRecommendation,\n  AIRecommendationResult,\n  ContextPack,\n)\n\n\nDEFAULT_CLAUDE_MODEL = \"claude-sonnet-4-20250514\"\nDEFAULT_SERVICE_TIMEOUT_SECONDS = 45.0\nDEFAULT_SERVICE_POLL_INTERVAL_SECONDS = 0.5\nCERTIFICATE_ERROR_MESSAGE = (\n  \"Network error: Python could not verify the TLS certificate for the AI provider. \"\n  \"This usually means your local Python certificate trust store is missing or outdated. \"\n  \"Update your OS or Python certificates, then try again. \"\n  \"RepoQuest did not disable SSL verification.\"\n)\n\n\ndef _load_local_env(env_path: str = \".env\") -> None:\n  \"\"\"Load simple KEY=value pairs from local.env files without extra dependencies.\"\"\"\n  candidates = [\n    Path(env_path),\n    Path(__file__).resolve().parents[1] / env_path,\n  ]\n\n  path = next((candidate for candidate in candidates if candidate.exists()), None)\n  if path is None:\n    return\n\n  for raw_line in path.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\", errors=\"ignore\").splitlines():\n    line = raw_line.strip()\n    if not line or line.startswith(\"#\") or \"=\" not in line:\n      continue\n\n    key, value = line.split(\"=\", 1)\n    key = key.strip()\n    value = value.strip().strip('\"').strip(\"'\")\n    if key and not os.environ.get(key):\n      os.environ[key] = value\n\n\ndef _streamlit_secret(name: str) -> object | None:\n  \"\"\"Read a Streamlit secret if Streamlit is available and configured.\"\"\"\n  try:\n    import streamlit as st\n\n    if hasattr(st, \"secrets\") and name in st.secrets:\n      return st.secrets[name]\n  except (ImportError, FileNotFoundError, KeyError):\n    return None\n  return None\n\n\ndef _is_truthy(value: object | None) -> bool:\n  \"\"\"Parse bool-like environment/secret values.\"\"\"\n  if isinstance(value, bool):\n    return value\n  if value is None:\n    return False\n  return str(value).strip().lower() in {\"1\", \"true\", \"yes\", \"on\"}\n\n\ndef get_assistant_config() -> tuple[bool, str, str, str, str, str]:\n  \"\"\"Return assistant enabled flag, provider type, Claude API key, Claude model, local base URL, and local model name.\"\"\"\n  _load_local_env()\n\n  enabled_value = os.getenv(\"REPOQUEST_AI_ENABLED\")\n  if enabled_value is None:\n    enabled_value = _streamlit_secret(\"REPOQUEST_AI_ENABLED\")\n\n  provider = os.getenv(\"REPOQUEST_ASSISTANT_PROVIDER\", \"\").strip().lower()\n  if not provider:\n    secret_provider = _streamlit_secret(\"REPOQUEST_ASSISTANT_PROVIDER\")\n    provider = str(secret_provider or \"\").strip().lower()\n  if not provider:\n    provider = \"claude\"\n\n  api_key = os.getenv(\"CLAUDE_API_KEY\", \"\").strip()\n  if not api_key:\n    secret_key = _streamlit_secret(\"CLAUDE_API_KEY\")\n    api_key = str(secret_key or \"\").strip()\n\n  model = os.getenv(\"CLAUDE_MODEL\", \"\").strip()\n  if not model:\n    secret_model = _streamlit_secret(\"CLAUDE_MODEL\")"

      "diff": "\n# Search: |||\n# Made with Bob\n|||\n# Start Line: 73\n# Replace with: |||\n# Deployment profile detection\ndef get_deployment_profile() -> str:\n    \"\"\"\n    Detect the current deployment profile based on environment configuration.\n    \n    Returns one of:\n    - \"deterministic\" - Default, no AI, no secrets required\n    - \"mock_assistant\" - AI enabled with mock provider for testing\n    - \"cloud_assistant\" - AI enabled with Claude/cloud provider\n    - \"local_model\" - AI enabled with local model provider\n    - \"service_assistant\" - AI enabled via async assistant service\n    \"\"\"\n    import os\n    \n    # Check if AI is enabled\n    ai_enabled = os.getenv(\"REPOQUEST_AI_ENABLED\", \"false\").strip().lower() in {\"true\", \"1\", \"yes\", \"on\"}\n    \n    if not ai_enabled:\n        return \"deterministic\"\n    \n    # Check for assistant service URL\n    service_url = os.getenv(\"REPOQUEST_ASSISTANT_SERVICE_URL\", \"\").strip()\n    if service_url:\n        # Service mode - check service provider\n        service_provider = os.getenv(\"REPOQUEST_ASSISTANT_SERVICE_PROVIDER\", \"\").strip().lower()\n        if service_provider == \"mock\":\n            return \"mock_assistant\"\n        return \"service_assistant\"\n    \n    # Direct provider mode\n    provider = os.getenv(\"REPOQUEST_ASSISTANT_PROVIDER\", \"\").strip().lower()\n    \n    if provider == \"mock\":\n        return \"mock_assistant\"\n    elif provider == \"local\":\n        return \"local_model\"\n    elif provider in {\"claude\", \"\"}:\n        # Default to cloud if API key is present\n        api_key = os.getenv(\"CLAUDE_API_KEY\", \"\").strip()\n        if api_key:\n            return \"cloud_assistant\"\n        return \"deterministic\"\n    \n    return \"deterministic\"\n\n\ndef get_profile_description(profile: str) -> str:\n    \"\"\"Get a human-friendly description of the deployment profile.\"\"\"\n    descriptions = {\n        \"deterministic\": \"Deterministic Code Assistant (no AI, no secrets required)\",\n        \"mock_assistant\": \"Mock AI Assistant (testing mode, no network calls)\",\n        \"cloud_assistant\": \"Cloud AI Assistant (Claude API)\",\n        \"local_model\": \"Local Model Assistant (private/local inference)\",\n        \"service_assistant\": \"Async Assistant Service (separate service container)\",\n    }\n    return descriptions.get(profile, \"Unknown profile\")\n\n\n# Made with Bob\n|||\n# Purpose: Add deployment profile detection functions\n"

IMPORTANT: NEVER use apply_diff to replace large parts or whole file contents of a file!! use write_to_file instead.\n      \n      Diff format:\n      ```\n# Search: |||\n[exact content to find including whitespace]\n|||\n# Start Line: [optional line number where search should begin]\n# Replace with: |||\n[new content to replace with]\n|||\n# Purpose: [optional explanation of what this replacement does]\n\n# Search: |||\n[another exact content to find]\n|||\n# Start Line: [optional line number]\n# Replace with: |||\n[replacement content]\n|||\n```\n\nFor multiline search or replacements, use triple |||, use another if content contains that delimiter:\n\n```\n# Search: |||\nfunction example() {\n  const x = 1;\n  const y = 2;\n  return x + y;\n}\n|||\n# Start Line: 10\n# Replace with: |||\nfunction example() {\n  const x = 1;\n  const y = 2;\n  console.log('Computing sum');\n  return x + y;\n}\n|||\n# Purpose: Add logging to the function\n```\n\nExample:\n\nOriginal file:\n```\n1 | def calculate_total(items):\n2 |     total = 0\n3 |     for item in items:\n4 |         total += item\n5 |     return total\n```\n\nSearch/Replace content:\n```\n# Search: |||\ntotal += item\n|||\n# Start Line: 3\n# Replace with: |||\ntotal = total + item\n|||\n# Purpose: Make addition more explicit\n\n# Search: |||\ndef calculate_total(items):\n|||\n# Replace with: |||\ndef calculate_sum(items):\n|||\n# Purpose: Rename function to better reflect its purpose\n```\n\nUsage:\n<apply_diff>\n<file_path>File path here</file_path>\n<diff>\nYour search/replace content here\nYou can use multiple search/replace blocks in one diff block\n</diff>\n</apply_diff>\n",

    "description": "Replace the entire TODO list with an updated checklist reflecting the current state. Always provide the full list; the system will overwrite the previous one. This tool is designed for step-by-step task tracking, allowing you to confirm completion of each step before updating, update multiple task statuses at once (e.g., mark one as completed and start the next), and dynamically add new todos discovered during long or complex tasks.\n\n**Checklist Format:**\n- Use a single-level markdown checklist (no nesting or subtasks).\n- List todos in the intended execution order.\n- Status options:\n\t - [ ] Task description (pending)\n\t - [x] Task description (completed)\n\t - [-] Task description (in progress)\n\n**Status Rules:**\n- [ ] = pending (not started)\n- [x] = completed (fully finished, no unresolved issues)\n- [-] = in_progress (currently being worked on)\n\n**Core Principles:**\n- Before updating, always confirm which todos have been completed since the last update.\n- You may update multiple statuses in a single update (e.g., mark the previous as completed and the next as in progress).\n- When a new actionable item is discovered during a long or complex task, add it to the todo list immediately.\n- Do not remove any unfinished todos unless explicitly instructed.\n- Always retain all unfinished tasks, updating their status as needed.\n- Only mark a task as completed when it is fully accomplished (no partials, no unresolved dependencies).\n- If a task is blocked, keep it as in_progress and add a new todo describing what needs to be resolved.\n- Remove tasks only if they are no longer relevant or if the user requests deletion.\n\n**Usage Example:**\n<update_todo_list>\n<todos>\n[x] Analyze requirements\n[x] Design architecture\n[-] Implement core logic\n[ ] Write tests\n[ ] Update documentation\n</todos>\n</update_todo_list>\n\n*After completing \"Implement core logic\" and starting \"Write tests\":*\n<update_todo_list>\n<todos>\n[x] Analyze requirements\n[x] Design architecture\n[x] Implement core logic\n[-] Write tests\n[ ] Update documentation\n[ ] Add performance benchmarks\n</todos>\n</update_todo_list>\n\n**When to Use:**\n- The task is complicated or involves multiple steps or requires ongoing tracking.\n- You need to update the status of several todos at once.\n- New actionable items are discovered during task execution.\n- The user requests a todo list or provides multiple tasks.\n- The task is complex and benefits from clear, stepwise progress tracking.\n\n**When NOT to Use:**\n- There is only a single, trivial task.\n- The task can be completed in one or two simple steps.\n- The request is purely conversational or informational.\n\n**Task Management Guidelines:**\n- Mark task as completed immediately after all work of the current task is done.\n- Start the next task by marking it as in_progress.\n- Add new todos as soon as they are identified.\n- Use clear, descriptive task names.\n",
    "renderOutputAsMarkdown": true
  }
]
```

Deterministic Code Assistant (default)\n#    - No AI, no secrets required\n#    - REPOQUEST_AI_ENABLED=false (or unset)\n#\n# 2. Mock AI Assistant (testing)\n#    - REPOQUEST_AI_ENABLED=true\n#    - REPOQUEST_ASSISTANT_PROVIDER=mock\n#\n# 3. Cloud AI Assistant (Claude)\n#    - REPOQUEST_AI_ENABLED=true\n#    - CLAUDE_API_KEY=your_key_here\n#    - REPOQUEST_ASSISTANT_PROVIDER=claude (or unset)\n#\n# 4. Local Model Assistant\n#    - REPOQUEST_AI_ENABLED=true\n#    - REPOQUEST_ASSISTANT_PROVIDER=local\n#    - REPOQUEST_LOCAL_MODEL_BASE_URL=http://localhost:11434/v1\n#    - REPOQUEST_LOCAL_MODEL_NAME=llama3.1\n#\n# 5. IMPORTANT: NEVER use apply_diff to replace large parts or whole file contents of a file!! use write_to_file instead.\n      \n      Diff format:\n      ```\n# Search: |||\n[exact content to find including whitespace]\n|||\n# Start Line: [optional line number where search should begin]\n# Replace with: |||\n[new content to replace with]\n|||\n# Purpose: [optional explanation of what this replacement does]\n\n# Search: |||\n[another exact content to find]\n|||\n# Start Line: [optional line number]\n# Replace with: |||\n[replacement content]\n|||\n```\n\nFor multiline search or replacements, use triple |||, use another if content contains that delimiter:\n\n```\n# Search: |||\nfunction example() {\n  const x = 1;\n  const y = 2;\n  return x + y;\n}\n|||\n# Start Line: 10\n# Replace with: |||\nfunction example() {\n  const x = 1;\n  const y = 2;\n  console.log('Computing sum');\n  return x + y;\n}\n|||\n# Purpose: Add logging to the function\n```\n\nExample:\n\nOriginal file:\n```\n1 | def calculate_total(items):\n2 |     total = 0\n3 |     for item in items:\n4 |         total += item\n5 |     return total\n```\n\nSearch/Replace content:\n```\n# Search: |||\ntotal += item\n|||\n# Start Line: 3\n# Replace with: |||\ntotal = total + item\n|||\n# Purpose: Make addition more explicit\n\n# Search: |||\ndef calculate_total(items):\n|||\n# Replace with: |||\ndef calculate_sum(items):\n|||\n# Purpose: Rename function to better reflect its purpose\n```\n\nUsage:\n<apply_diff>\n<file_path>File path here</file_path>\n<diff>\nYour search/replace content here\nYou can use multiple search/replace blocks in one diff block\n</diff>\n</apply_diff>\n",
    "renderOutputAsMarkdown": true
  }
]

    "displayName": "To-do List",
    "description": "Replace the entire TODO list with an updated checklist reflecting the current state. Always provide the full list; the system will overwrite the previous one. This tool is designed for step-by-step task tracking, allowing you to confirm completion of each step before updating, update multiple task statuses at once (e.g., mark one as completed and start the next), and dynamically add new todos discovered during long or complex tasks.\n\n**Checklist Format:**\n- Use a single-level markdown checklist (no nesting or subtasks).\n- List todos in the intended execution order.\n- Status options:\n\t - [ ] Task description (pending)\n\t - [x] Task description (completed)\n\t - [-] Task description (in progress)\n\n**Status Rules:**\n- [ ] = pending (not started)\n- [x] = completed (fully finished, no unresolved issues)\n- [-] = in_progress (currently being worked on)\n\n**Core Principles:**\n- Before updating, always confirm which todos have been completed since the last update.\n- You may update multiple statuses in a single update (e.g., mark the previous as completed and the next as in progress).\n- When a new actionable item is discovered during a long or complex task, add it to the todo list immediately.\n- Do not remove any unfinished todos unless explicitly instructed.\n- Always retain all unfinished tasks, updating their status as needed.\n- Only mark a task as completed when it is fully accomplished (no partials, no unresolved dependencies).\n- If a task is blocked, keep it as in_progress and add a new todo describing what needs to be resolved.\n- Remove tasks only if they are no longer relevant or if the user requests deletion.\n\n**Usage Example:**\n<update_todo_list>\n<todos>\n[x] Analyze requirements\n[x] Design architecture\n[-] Implement core logic\n[ ] Write tests\n[ ] Update documentation\n</todos>\n</update_todo_list>\n\n*After completing \"Implement core logic\" and starting \"Write tests\":*\n<update_todo_list>\n<todos>\n[x] Analyze requirements\n[x] Design architecture\n[x] Implement core logic\n[-] Write tests\n[ ] Update documentation\n[ ] Add performance benchmarks\n</todos>\n</update_todo_list>\n\n**When to Use:**\n- The task is complicated or involves multiple steps or requires ongoing tracking.\n- You need to update the status of several todos at once.\n- New actionable items are discovered during task execution.\n- The user requests a todo list or provides multiple tasks.\n- The task is complex and benefits from clear, stepwise progress tracking.\n\n**When NOT to Use:**\n- There is only a single, trivial task.\n- The task can be completed in one or two simple steps.\n- The request is purely conversational or informational.\n\n**Task Management Guidelines:**\n- Mark task as completed immediately after all work of the current task is done.\n- Start the next task by marking it as in_progress.\n- Add new todos as soon as they are identified.\n- Use clear, descriptive task names.\n",
    "renderOutputAsMarkdown": true
  }
]
```

Timestamp: 2026-05-16T17:11:14.790Z

Model: premium

<thinking>**Create infra/docker/README.md**
Need to create comprehensive documentation for Docker deployment profiles with examples for each mode.
</thinking>

<details>
<summary>Tool calls</summary>

```json
[
  {
    "id": "tool-135",
    "name": "write_to_file",
    "args": {
      "file_path": "/home/ovidiu/Projects/bob/proj/infra/docker/README.md",
      "content": "# Docker Deployment Guide\n\nThis directory contains Docker configurations for RepoQuest deployment.\n\n## Available Dockerfiles\n\n- `Dockerfile.app` - Main Streamlit application\n- `Dockerfile.assistant` - Optional async assistant service\n\n## Deployment Profiles\n\nRepoQuest supports multiple deployment profiles via Docker Compose. Each profile is designed for specific use cases.\n\n### Profile 1: Deterministic Code Assistant (Default)\n\n**Use case:** Production demo, Streamlit Community Cloud alternative, no AI required\n\n**Features:**\n- No secrets required\n- No external API calls\n- Fully deterministic analysis\n- Safe for public deployment\n- Works offline\n\n**Command:**\n```bash\ndocker compose up --build repoquest\n```\n\n**Access:**\n- App: http://localhost:8501\n\n**Environment variables:** None required\n\n---\n\n### Profile 2: Mock AI Assistant\n\n**Use case:** Testing, development, CI/CD validation\n\n**Features:**\n- AI mode enabled\n- No network calls\n- Deterministic mock responses\n- Tests assistant UI without API costs\n- Validates async service integration\n\n**Command:**\n```bash\nREPOQUEST_AI_ENABLED=true \\\nREPOQUEST_ASSISTANT_SERVICE_URL=http://assistant:8765 \\\nREPOQUEST_ASSISTANT_SERVICE_PROVIDER=mock \\\ndocker compose --profile assistant up --build\n```\n\n**Access:**\n- App: http://localhost:8501\n- Assistant service: http://localhost:8765\n- Health check: http://localhost:8765/health\n\n**Environment variables:**\n- `REPOQUEST_AI_ENABLED=true` - Enable AI features\n- `REPOQUEST_ASSISTANT_SERVICE_URL=http://assistant:8765` - Point to service\n- `REPOQUEST_ASSISTANT_SERVICE_PROVIDER=mock` - Use mock provider\n\n---\n\n### Profile 3: Cloud AI Assistant (Claude)\n\n**Use case:** Live demo with AI insights, development with real provider\n\n**Features:**\n- Real Claude API integration\n- Async assistant service\n- Bounded context packs\n- Response validation\n- Deterministic fallback preserved\n\n**Command:**\n```bash\nREPOQUEST_AI_ENABLED=true \\\nREPOQUEST_ASSISTANT_SERVICE_URL=http://assistant:8765 \\\nCLAUDE_API_KEY=your_api_key_here \\\nCLAUDE_MODEL=claude-sonnet-4-20250514 \\\ndocker compose --profile assistant up --build\n```\n\n**Access:**\n- App: http://localhost:8501\n- Assistant service: http://localhost:8765\n- Health check: http://localhost:8765/health\n\n**Environment variables:**\n- `REPOQUEST_AI_ENABLED=true` - Enable AI features\n- `REPOQUEST_ASSISTANT_SERVICE_URL=http://assistant:8765` - Point to service\n- `CLAUDE_API_KEY=your_key` - Your Anthropic API key\n- `CLAUDE_MODEL=claude-sonnet-4-20250514` - Model to use (optional)\n\n**Requirements:**\n- Valid Anthropic API key\n- Network access to api.anthropic.com\n- API usage costs apply\n\n**Privacy note:** Code snippets (max 800 chars each) are sent to Claude API. See `docs/assistant_mode.md` for details.\n\n---\n\n### Profile 4: Local Model Assistant\n\n**Use case:** Private/offline AI, local model experimentation, no cloud dependencies\n\n**Features:**\n- No cloud API required\n- Works with local model servers\n- OpenAI-compatible endpoint support\n- Bounded context packs\n- Response validation\n- Deterministic fallback preserved\n\n**Supported local model servers:**\n- Ollama (recommended)\n- LM Studio\n- llama.cpp server\n- Any OpenAI-compatible endpoint\n\n**Setup Ollama example:**\n\n1. Install and start Ollama on host:\n```bash\n# Install Ollama (macOS/Linux)\ncurl -fsSL https://ollama.com/install.sh | sh\n\n# Pull a model\nollama pull llama3.1\n\n# Verify it's running\ncurl http://localhost:11434/v1/models\n```\n\n2. Run RepoQuest with local model:\n```bash\nREPOQUEST_AI_ENABLED=true \\\nREPOQUEST_ASSISTANT_SERVICE_URL=http://assistant:8765 \\\nREPOQUEST_ASSISTANT_SERVICE_PROVIDER=local \\\nREPOQUEST_LOCAL_MODEL_BASE_URL=http://host.docker.internal:11434/v1 \\\nREPOQUEST_LOCAL_MODEL_NAME=llama3.1 \\\ndocker compose --profile assistant up --build\n```\n\n**Access:**\n- App: http://localhost:8501\n- Assistant service: http://localhost:8765\n- Health check: http://localhost:8765/health\n\n**Environment variables:**\n- `REPOQUEST_AI_ENABLED=true` - Enable AI features\n- `REPOQUEST_ASSISTANT_SERVICE_URL=http://assistant:8765` - Point to service\n- `REPOQUEST_ASSISTANT_SERVICE_PROVIDER=local` - Use local model provider\n- `REPOQUEST_LOCAL_MODEL_BASE_URL=http://host.docker.internal:11434/v1` - Local server URL\n- `REPOQUEST_LOCAL_MODEL_NAME=llama3.1` - Model name\n\n**Notes:**\n- `host.docker.internal` allows Docker containers to access host services\n- Local model responses may be slower than cloud APIs\n- Model quality varies by size and training\n- No API costs, but requires local compute resources\n\n---\n\n## Direct Provider Mode (No Service)\n\nYou can also run the app with direct provider calls (no async service):\n\n**Mock mode:**\n```bash\nREPOQUEST_AI_ENABLED=true \\\nREPOQUEST_ASSISTANT_PROVIDER=mock \\\ndocker compose up --build repoquest\n```\n\n**Claude mode:**\n```bash\nREPOQUEST_AI_ENABLED=true \\\nREPOQUEST_ASSISTANT_PROVIDER=claude \\\nCLAUDE_API_KEY=your_key_here \\\ndocker compose up --build repoquest\n```\n\n**Local model mode:**\n```bash\nREPOQUEST_AI_ENABLED=true \\\nREPOQUEST_ASSISTANT_PROVIDER=local \\\nREPOQUEST_LOCAL_MODEL_BASE_URL=http://host.docker.internal:11434/v1 \\\nREPOQUEST_LOCAL_MODEL_NAME=llama3.1 \\\ndocker compose up --build repoquest\n```\n\n---\n\n## Environment Variables Reference\n\n### Core Settings\n\n| Variable | Default | Description |\n|----------|---------|-------------|\n| `REPOQUEST_AI_ENABLED` | `false` | Enable AI assistant features |\n\n### Assistant Service Settings\n\n| Variable | Default | Description |\n|----------|---------|-------------|\n| `REPOQUEST_ASSISTANT_SERVICE_URL` | (empty) | Assistant service URL (e.g., `http://assistant:8765`) |\n| `REPOQUEST_ASSISTANT_SERVICE_TIMEOUT_SECONDS` | `45` | Request timeout for service calls |\n| `REPOQUEST_ASSISTANT_SERVICE_PROVIDER` | (empty) | Provider override for service: `mock`, `claude`, `local` |\n\n### Direct Provider Settings\n\n| Variable | Default | Description |\n|----------|---------|-------------|\n| `REPOQUEST_ASSISTANT_PROVIDER` | `claude` | Direct provider: `mock`, `claude`, `local` |\n\n### Cloud Provider Settings (Claude)\n\n| Variable | Default | Description |\n|----------|---------|-------------|\n| `CLAUDE_API_KEY` | (empty) | Anthropic API key |\n| `CLAUDE_MODEL` | `claude-sonnet-4-20250514` | Claude model to use |\n\n### Local Model Settings\n\n| Variable | Default | Description |\n|----------|---------|-------------|\n| `REPOQUEST_LOCAL_MODEL_BASE_URL` | (empty) | OpenAI-compatible endpoint URL |\n| `REPOQUEST_LOCAL_MODEL_NAME` | (empty) | Model name to request |\n\n---\n\n## Troubleshooting\n\n### Assistant service won't start\n\n**Symptom:** `docker compose --profile assistant up` fails\n\n**Solutions:**\n1. Check that `REPOQUEST_AI_ENABLED=true` is set\n2. Verify Docker Compose version supports profiles (v1.28+)\n3. Check logs: `docker compose --profile assistant logs assistant`\n\n### App can't reach assistant service\n\n**Symptom:** \"Assistant service network error\" in UI\n\n**Solutions:**\n1. Verify service is running: `docker compose --profile assistant ps`\n2. Check health endpoint: `curl http://localhost:8765/health`\n3. Verify `REPOQUEST_ASSISTANT_SERVICE_URL=http://assistant:8765` in app container\n4. Check Docker network: `docker network inspect proj_default`\n\n### Local model connection fails\n\n**Symptom:** \"Local model server unavailable\" errors\n\n**Solutions:**\n1. Verify Ollama/model server is running on host\n2. Test from host: `curl http://localhost:11434/v1/models`\n3. Check `host.docker.internal` resolves: `docker compose exec assistant ping host.docker.internal`\n4. Try explicit host IP instead of `host.docker.internal`\n5. Verify model is pulled: `ollama list`\n\n### Claude API errors\n\n**Symptom:** \"Claude API error\" or rate limit messages\n\n**Solutions:**\n1. Verify API key is valid\n2. Check Anthropic API status\n3. Review rate limits in Anthropic console\n4. Try mock mode first to verify setup\n\n### Slow responses\n\n**Symptom:** AI requests take a long time\n\n**Expected behavior:**\n- Mock mode: < 1 second\n- Claude API: 2-5 seconds typical\n- Local models: 5-30 seconds depending on hardware and model size\n\n**Solutions for local models:**\n1. Use smaller models (e.g., `llama3.1:8b` instead of `llama3.1:70b`)\n2. Ensure adequate RAM/VRAM\n3. Check CPU/GPU utilization\n4. Consider quantized models for faster inference\n\n---\n\n## Security Notes\n\n### Secrets Management\n\n**Never commit secrets to git:**\n- Use `.env` files (gitignored)\n- Use Docker secrets for production\n- Use Streamlit secrets for cloud deployment\n\n**In Docker Compose:**\n```bash\n# Good: Environment variable from shell\nCLAUDE_API_KEY=your_key docker compose up\n\n# Good: .env file (gitignored)\ndocker compose --env-file .env up\n\n# Bad: Hardcoded in docker-compose.yml\n# environment:\n#   CLAUDE_API_KEY: \"sk-ant-...\"  # DON'T DO THIS\n```\n\n### Network Security\n\n**Deterministic profile:**\n- No external network calls\n- Safe for public deployment\n- No secrets required\n\n**AI-enabled profiles:**\n- Cloud mode: Sends code snippets to external API\n- Local mode: Network calls stay on local network\n- Service mode: Internal Docker network only\n\n### Data Privacy\n\n**What gets sent to AI providers:**\n- Capped code snippets (max 800 chars each)\n- File paths and roles\n- Project metadata\n- Detected routes/components\n\n**Never sent:**\n- Full file contents\n- Binary files\n- `.env` files\n- Secrets or credentials\n- User data\n\nSee `docs/assistant_mode.md` for complete privacy details.\n\n---\n\n## Production Deployment\n\n### Streamlit Community Cloud\n\nFor Streamlit Cloud deployment, use the deterministic profile (no Docker required):\n\n1. Push repo to GitHub\n2. Deploy via Streamlit Cloud dashboard\n3. Set main file: `app/streamlit_app.py`\n4. No secrets required for deterministic mode\n\nFor AI-enabled cloud deployment:\n1. Add secrets in Streamlit dashboard:\n   ```toml\n   REPOQUEST_AI_ENABLED = \"true\"\n   CLAUDE_API_KEY = \"your_key_here\"\n   ```\n2. Use direct provider mode (no assistant service)\n\n### Self-Hosted with Docker\n\n**Deterministic only:**\n```bash\ndocker compose up -d repoquest\n```\n\n**With AI assistant service:**\n```bash\n# Create .env file with secrets\ndocker compose --profile assistant up -d\n```\n\n**Behind reverse proxy:**\n```nginx\nlocation / {\n    proxy_pass http://localhost:8501;\n    proxy_http_version 1.1;\n    proxy_set_header Upgrade $http_upgrade;\n    proxy_set_header Connection \"upgrade\";\n    proxy_set_header Host $host;\n}\n```\n\n---\n\n## Development\n\n### Building Images\n\n```bash\n# Build app only\ndocker compose build repoquest\n\n# Build app and assistant service\ndocker compose --profile assistant build\n```\n\n### Running Tests\n\n```bash\n# Run tests in container\ndocker compose run --rm repoquest pytest -q\n\n# Run with coverage\ndocker compose run --rm repoquest pytest --cov=repoquest\n```\n\n### Debugging\n\n```bash\n# View logs\ndocker compose logs -f repoquest\ndocker compose --profile assistant logs -f assistant\n\n# Shell into container\ndocker compose exec repoquest bash\ndocker compose exec assistant bash\n\n# Inspect environment\ndocker compose exec repoquest env | grep REPOQUEST\n```\n\n---\n\n## Support\n\nFor issues or questions:\n\n1. Check this documentation\n2. Review `docs/assistant_mode.md`\n3. Check Docker Compose logs\n4. Test with mock mode first\n5. Open GitHub issue with details\n\n---\n\n**Remember:** The deterministic profile is the default and safest option. AI features are optional enhancements.\n"
    },

]

    "description": "Replace the entire TODO list with an updated checklist reflecting the current state. Always provide the full list; the system will overwrite the previous one. This tool is designed for step-by-step task tracking, allowing you to confirm completion of each step before updating, update multiple task statuses at once (e.g., mark one as completed and start the next), and dynamically add new todos discovered during long or complex tasks.\n\n**Checklist Format:**\n- Use a single-level markdown checklist (no nesting or subtasks).\n- List todos in the intended execution order.\n- Status options:\n\t - [ ] Task description (pending)\n\t - [x] Task description (completed)\n\t - [-] Task description (in progress)\n\n**Status Rules:**\n- [ ] = pending (not started)\n- [x] = completed (fully finished, no unresolved issues)\n- [-] = in_progress (currently being worked on)\n\n**Core Principles:**\n- Before updating, always confirm which todos have been completed since the last update.\n- You may update multiple statuses in a single update (e.g., mark the previous as completed and the next as in progress).\n- When a new actionable item is discovered during a long or complex task, add it to the todo list immediately.\n- Do not remove any unfinished todos unless explicitly instructed.\n- Always retain all unfinished tasks, updating their status as needed.\n- Only mark a task as completed when it is fully accomplished (no partials, no unresolved dependencies).\n- If a task is blocked, keep it as in_progress and add a new todo describing what needs to be resolved.\n- Remove tasks only if they are no longer relevant or if the user requests deletion.\n\n**Usage Example:**\n<update_todo_list>\n<todos>\n[x] Analyze requirements\n[x] Design architecture\n[-] Implement core logic\n[ ] Write tests\n[ ] Update documentation\n</todos>\n</update_todo_list>\n\n*After completing \"Implement core logic\" and starting \"Write tests\":*\n<update_todo_list>\n<todos>\n[x] Analyze requirements\n[x] Design architecture\n[x] Implement core logic\n[-] Write tests\n[ ] Update documentation\n[ ] Add performance benchmarks\n</todos>\n</update_todo_list>\n\n**When to Use:**\n- The task is complicated or involves multiple steps or requires ongoing tracking.\n- You need to update the status of several todos at once.\n- New actionable items are discovered during task execution.\n- The user requests a todo list or provides multiple tasks.\n- The task is complex and benefits from clear, stepwise progress tracking.\n\n**When NOT to Use:**\n- There is only a single, trivial task.\n- The task can be completed in one or two simple steps.\n- The request is purely conversational or informational.\n\n**Task Management Guidelines:**\n- Mark task as completed immediately after all work of the current task is done.\n- Start the next task by marking it as in_progress.\n- Add new todos as soon as they are identified.\n- Use clear, descriptive task names.\n",
    "renderOutputAsMarkdown": true
  }
]

[
  {
    "id": "tool-137",
    "name": "apply_diff",
    "args": {
      "file_path": "/home/ovidiu/Projects/bob/proj/docs/assistant_mode.md",
      "diff": "\n# Search: |||\n## Overview\n\nRepoQuest includes an **optional AI assistant** powered by Claude that can provide additional insights beyond deterministic static analysis.\n\n**Important:** The AI assistant is:\n- **Disabled by default**\n- **Optional** - all core RepoQuest features work without it\n- **Manual-only** - never runs automatically\n- **Bounded** - only receives capped context packs, not entire repos\n- **Safe** - validates responses to prevent execution claims\n|||\n# Replace with: |||\n## Overview\n\nRepoQuest includes an **optional AI assistant** that can provide additional insights beyond deterministic static analysis.\n\n**Important:** The AI assistant is:\n- **Disabled by default**\n- **Optional** - all core RepoQuest features work without it\n- **Manual-only** - never runs automatically\n- **Bounded** - only receives capped context packs, not entire repos\n- **Safe** - validates responses to prevent execution claims\n- **Multiple providers** - supports Claude, local models, and mock mode\n\n## Deployment Profiles\n\nRepoQuest supports multiple deployment profiles to accommodate different use cases, from public demos to private local development.\n\n### Profile Comparison\n\n| Profile | AI Enabled | Secrets Required | Network Calls | Use Case |\n|---------|-----------|------------------|---------------|----------|\n| **Deterministic** | No | None | None | Default, public demo, Streamlit Cloud |\n| **Mock Assistant** | Yes | None | None | Testing, CI/CD, development |\n| **Cloud Assistant** | Yes | API key | Claude API | Live demo with AI, development |\n| **Local Model** | Yes | None | Local only | Private/offline AI, experimentation |\n| **Service Assistant** | Yes | Varies | Varies | Async processing, scalability |\n\n### 1. Deterministic Code Assistant (Default)\n\n**Status:** Production-ready, recommended for public deployment\n\n**Features:**\n- No AI, no secrets, no external calls\n- Fully deterministic static analysis\n- Safe for Streamlit Community Cloud\n- Works offline\n- Zero API costs\n\n**Setup:**\n```bash\n# Local\npython scripts/run_local.py\n\n# Docker\ndocker compose up --build repoquest\n\n# Streamlit Cloud\n# Deploy with no secrets required\n```\n\n**Environment:**\n```bash\nREPOQUEST_AI_ENABLED=false  # or unset\n```\n\n### 2. Mock AI Assistant\n\n**Status:** Development/testing only\n\n**Features:**\n- AI UI enabled with deterministic responses\n- No network calls\n- No API costs\n- Tests assistant integration\n- Validates UI flows\n\n**Setup:**\n```bash\n# Local\nREPOQUEST_AI_ENABLED=true \\\nREPOQUEST_ASSISTANT_PROVIDER=mock \\\npython scripts/run_local.py\n\n# Docker with service\nREPOQUEST_AI_ENABLED=true \\\nREPOQUEST_ASSISTANT_SERVICE_URL=http://assistant:8765 \\\nREPOQUEST_ASSISTANT_SERVICE_PROVIDER=mock \\\ndocker compose --profile assistant up --build\n```\n\n**Environment:**\n```bash\nREPOQUEST_AI_ENABLED=true\nREPOQUEST_ASSISTANT_PROVIDER=mock\n```\n\n### 3. Cloud AI Assistant (Claude)\n\n**Status:** Production-ready with API key\n\n**Features:**\n- Real Claude API integration\n- High-quality AI insights\n- Bounded context packs\n- Response validation\n- Deterministic fallback preserved\n\n**Setup:**\n```bash\n# Local\nREPOQUEST_AI_ENABLED=true \\\nCLAUDE_API_KEY=your_key_here \\\npython scripts/run_local.py\n\n# Docker with service\nREPOQUEST_AI_ENABLED=true \\\nREPOQUEST_ASSISTANT_SERVICE_URL=http://assistant:8765 \\\nCLAUDE_API_KEY=your_key_here \\\ndocker compose --profile assistant up --build\n\n# Streamlit Cloud\n# Add secrets in dashboard:\n# REPOQUEST_AI_ENABLED = \"true\"\n# CLAUDE_API_KEY = \"your_key_here\"\n```\n\n**Environment:**\n```bash\nREPOQUEST_AI_ENABLED=true\nCLAUDE_API_KEY=your_api_key_here\nCLAUDE_MODEL=claude-sonnet-4-20250514  # optional\n```\n\n**Requirements:**\n- Valid Anthropic API key\n- Network access to api.anthropic.com\n- API usage costs apply\n\n**Privacy:** Code snippets (max 800 chars each) sent to Claude API. See Privacy & Data section.\n\n### 4. Local Model Assistant\n\n**Status:** Experimental, for private/offline use\n\n**Features:**\n- No cloud API required\n- Works with local model servers\n- OpenAI-compatible endpoint support\n- Private/offline AI\n- No API costs (compute costs only)\n\n**Supported servers:**\n- Ollama (recommended)\n- LM Studio\n- llama.cpp server\n- Any OpenAI-compatible endpoint\n\n**Setup with Ollama:**\n```bash\n# 1. Install and start Ollama\ncurl -fsSL https://ollama.com/install.sh | sh\nollama pull llama3.1\n\n# 2. Run RepoQuest\nREPOQUEST_AI_ENABLED=true \\\nREPOQUEST_ASSISTANT_PROVIDER=local \\\nREPOQUEST_LOCAL_MODEL_BASE_URL=http://localhost:11434/v1 \\\nREPOQUEST_LOCAL_MODEL_NAME=llama3.1 \\\npython scripts/run_local.py\n\n# 3. Docker with service\nREPOQUEST_AI_ENABLED=true \\\nREPOQUEST_ASSISTANT_SERVICE_URL=http://assistant:8765 \\\nREPOQUEST_ASSISTANT_SERVICE_PROVIDER=local \\\nREPOQUEST_LOCAL_MODEL_BASE_URL=http://host.docker.internal:11434/v1 \\\nREPOQUEST_LOCAL_MODEL_NAME=llama3.1 \\\ndocker compose --profile assistant up --build\n```\n\n**Environment:**\n```bash\nREPOQUEST_AI_ENABLED=true\nREPOQUEST_ASSISTANT_PROVIDER=local\nREPOQUEST_LOCAL_MODEL_BASE_URL=http://localhost:11434/v1\nREPOQUEST_LOCAL_MODEL_NAME=llama3.1\n```\n\n**Notes:**\n- Local model quality varies by size and training\n- Responses may be slower than cloud APIs\n- Requires local compute resources\n- No data leaves your machine\n\n### 5. Async Assistant Service\n\n**Status:** Production-ready for scalability\n\n**Features:**\n- Separate service container\n- Async job processing\n- Keeps Streamlit UI responsive\n- Supports all provider types\n- In-memory job queue\n\n**Setup:**\n```bash\n# Mock mode\nREPOQUEST_AI_ENABLED=true \\\nREPOQUEST_ASSISTANT_SERVICE_URL=http://assistant:8765 \\\nREPOQUEST_ASSISTANT_SERVICE_PROVIDER=mock \\\ndocker compose --profile assistant up --build\n\n# Cloud mode\nREPOQUEST_AI_ENABLED=true \\\nREPOQUEST_ASSISTANT_SERVICE_URL=http://assistant:8765 \\\nCLAUDE_API_KEY=your_key_here \\\ndocker compose --profile assistant up --build\n\n# Local model mode\nREPOQUEST_AI_ENABLED=true \\\nREPOQUEST_ASSISTANT_SERVICE_URL=http://assistant:8765 \\\nREPOQUEST_ASSISTANT_SERVICE_PROVIDER=local \\\nREPOQUEST_LOCAL_MODEL_BASE_URL=http://host.docker.internal:11434/v1 \\\nREPOQUEST_LOCAL_MODEL_NAME=llama3.1 \\\ndocker compose --profile assistant up --build\n```\n\n**Environment:**\n```bash\nREPOQUEST_AI_ENABLED=true\nREPOQUEST_ASSISTANT_SERVICE_URL=http://assistant:8765\nREPOQUEST_ASSISTANT_SERVICE_TIMEOUT_SECONDS=45\n# Plus provider-specific variables\n```\n\n**Service endpoints:**\n- `GET /health` - Health check\n- `POST /jobs` - Submit assistant request\n- `GET /jobs/{job_id}` - Poll job status\n\n**Notes:**\n- Jobs stored in memory only\n- Restarting service clears pending jobs\n- Deterministic analysis unaffected by service status\n|||\n# Purpose: Add comprehensive deployment profiles section\n"
    },

    "description": "Use to apply multiple PRECISE, TARGETED and SMALL modifications to one or more files by searching for specific sections of content and replacing them. apply_diff always operates on *whole* lines. the search must have exact same spacing, indentation and text. IMPORTANT: NEVER use apply_diff to replace large parts or whole file contents of a file!! use write_to_file instead.\n      \n      Diff format:\n      ```\n# Search: |||\n[exact content to find including whitespace]\n|||\n# Start Line: [optional line number where search should begin]\n# Replace with: |||\n[new content to replace with]\n|||\n# Purpose: [optional explanation of what this replacement does]\n\n# Search: |||\n[another exact content to find]\n|||\n# Start Line: [optional line number]\n# Replace with: |||\n[replacement content]\n|||\n```\n\nFor multiline search or replacements, use triple |||, use another if content contains that delimiter:\n\n```\n# Search: |||\nfunction example() {\n  const x = 1;\n  const y = 2;\n  return x + y;\n}\n|||\n# Start Line: 10\n# Replace with: |||\nfunction example() {\n  const x = 1;\n  const y = 2;\n  console.log('Computing sum');\n  return x + y;\n}\n|||\n# Purpose: Add logging to the function\n```\n\nExample:\n\nOriginal file:\n```\n1 | def calculate_total(items):\n2 |     total = 0\n3 |     for item in items:\n4 |         total += item\n5 |     return total\n```\n\nSearch/Replace content:\n```\n# Search: |||\ntotal += item\n|||\n# Start Line: 3\n# Replace with: |||\ntotal = total + item\n|||\n# Purpose: Make addition more explicit\n\n# Search: |||\ndef calculate_total(items):\n|||\n# Replace with: |||\ndef calculate_sum(items):\n|||\n# Purpose: Rename function to better reflect its purpose\n```\n\nUsage:\n<apply_diff>\n<file_path>File path here</file_path>\n<diff>\nYour search/replace content here\nYou can use multiple search/replace blocks in one diff block\n</diff>\n</apply_diff>\n",
    "renderOutputAsMarkdown": true
  }
]

    "timestamp": "2026-05-16T17:11:51.285Z",
    "displayName": "To-do List",
    "description": "Replace the entire TODO list with an updated checklist reflecting the current state. Always provide the full list; the system will overwrite the previous one. This tool is designed for step-by-step task tracking, allowing you to confirm completion of each step before updating, update multiple task statuses at once (e.g., mark one as completed and start the next), and dynamically add new todos discovered during long or complex tasks.\n\n**Checklist Format:**\n- Use a single-level markdown checklist (no nesting or subtasks).\n- List todos in the intended execution order.\n- Status options:\n\t - [ ] Task description (pending)\n\t - [x] Task description (completed)\n\t - [-] Task description (in progress)\n\n**Status Rules:**\n- [ ] = pending (not started)\n- [x] = completed (fully finished, no unresolved issues)\n- [-] = in_progress (currently being worked on)\n\n**Core Principles:**\n- Before updating, always confirm which todos have been completed since the last update.\n- You may update multiple statuses in a single update (e.g., mark the previous as completed and the next as in progress).\n- When a new actionable item is discovered during a long or complex task, add it to the todo list immediately.\n- Do not remove any unfinished todos unless explicitly instructed.\n- Always retain all unfinished tasks, updating their status as needed.\n- Only mark a task as completed when it is fully accomplished (no partials, no unresolved dependencies).\n- If a task is blocked, keep it as in_progress and add a new todo describing what needs to be resolved.\n- Remove tasks only if they are no longer relevant or if the user requests deletion.\n\n**Usage Example:**\n<update_todo_list>\n<todos>\n[x] Analyze requirements\n[x] Design architecture\n[-] Implement core logic\n[ ] Write tests\n[ ] Update documentation\n</todos>\n</update_todo_list>\n\n*After completing \"Implement core logic\" and starting \"Write tests\":*\n<update_todo_list>\n<todos>\n[x] Analyze requirements\n[x] Design architecture\n[x] Implement core logic\n[-] Write tests\n[ ] Update documentation\n[ ] Add performance benchmarks\n</todos>\n</update_todo_list>\n\n**When to Use:**\n- The task is complicated or involves multiple steps or requires ongoing tracking.\n- You need to update the status of several todos at once.\n- New actionable items are discovered during task execution.\n- The user requests a todo list or provides multiple tasks.\n- The task is complex and benefits from clear, stepwise progress tracking.\n\n**When NOT to Use:**\n- There is only a single, trivial task.\n- The task can be completed in one or two simple steps.\n- The request is purely conversational or informational.\n\n**Task Management Guidelines:**\n- Mark task as completed immediately after all work of the current task is done.\n- Start the next task by marking it as in_progress.\n- Add new todos as soon as they are identified.\n- Use clear, descriptive task names.\n",
    "renderOutputAsMarkdown": true
  }
]
```

            "output": "\nIMPORTANT: The file content has been truncated.\nStatus: Showing lines 1-100 of 305 total lines.\nAction: To read more of the file, you can use the 'offset' and 'limit' parameters in a subsequent 'read_file' call. For example, to read the next section of the file, use offset: 100.\n\n--- FILE CONTENT (truncated) ---\n# RepoQuest\n\n**Turn unfamiliar repos into guided onboarding journeys**\n\nRepoQuest helps developers onboard to small codebases faster by converting repository structure into a guided quest: project fingerprint, application graph, reading workbench, component cards, test intelligence, work plans, quiz, exportable docs, and optional AI-assisted follow-up actions.\n\n## What is RepoQuest?\n\nRepoQuest is a deterministic repository onboarding mapper for small prototype-sized codebases. It analyzes repository structure and generates:\n\n- **Project Fingerprint** - Detects project type, frameworks, and entry points\n- **Architecture Map** - Visualizes component relationships and dependencies\n- **Reading Workbench** - Suggests a guided tour with capped, scrollable file previews\n- **Component Cards** - Detailed information about important files with test ideas\n- **Test Intelligence** - Maps tests to likely targets and suggests missing coverage\n- **Work Plans** - Generates deterministic epics, tasks, and milestones\n- **Agent Workflows** - Generates step-by-step workflows for IBM Bob or another coding assistant\n- **Onboarding Quiz** - Verifies understanding with interactive questions\n- **Markdown Export** - Generates comprehensive onboarding documentation\n\n## Why RepoQuest?\n\nDevelopers waste time figuring out where to start in unfamiliar codebases. RepoQuest solves this by:\n\n- **Fast Analysis** - Scans and analyzes repositories in seconds\n- **Deterministic Core** - Uses static analysis and rule evaluators; optional AI actions are disabled by default\n- **Safe** - Validates ZIP uploads, never executes uploaded code\n- **Actionable** - Provides specific next steps and optional AI Assistant actions\n- **Exportable** - Generates standalone Markdown guides for team onboarding\n\n## Features\n\n### Core Analysis\n- Framework detection (React, FastAPI, Flask, Express, Next.js, Django, Streamlit)\n- Project type classification (full-stack, frontend, backend, CLI, library)\n- Entry point identification\n- API route extraction (FastAPI supported in MVP 1)\n- Import/dependency graph generation with connected-node graph views\n- Test inventory, impact mapping, quality signals, and missing-coverage suggestions\n- Rule-driven deterministic indicators and reusable response templates\n\n### Onboarding Tools\n- Prioritized reading path with time estimates and inline file previews\n- Component cards with connections and test ideas\n- Deterministic epics, tasks, milestones, and follow-up workflows\n- Interactive quiz for knowledge verification\n- Documentation and config file previews\n- Optional AI Assistant actions for deeper exploration\n\n### Security\n- ZIP slip protection\n- Path traversal prevention\n- File size limits (25 MB max)\n- Binary file detection\n- No code execution\n- No dependency installation\n\n## Quick Start\n\n### Local Development\n\n**Prerequisites:**\n- Python 3.11 or higher\n- pip\n\n**Setup:**\n\n```bash\n# Clone the repository\ngit clone <repository-url>\ncd repoquest\n\n# Create virtual environment\npython -m venv .venv\n\n# Activate virtual environment\n# On Linux/Mac:\nsource .venv/bin/activate\n# On Windows:\n.venv\\Scripts\\Activate.ps1\n\n# Install dependencies\npip install -r infra/local/requirements-dev.txt\n\n# Run the app\npython scripts/run_local.py\n```\n\nThe app will open at `http://localhost:8501`\n\n**Run Tests:**\n\n```bash\npytest -q\n```\n\n**Lint Code:**\n\n```bash\nruff check ."
          }
        }
      }

          "response": {
            "output": "\nIMPORTANT: The file content has been truncated.\nStatus: Showing lines 101-200 of 305 total lines.\nAction: To read more of the file, you can use the 'offset' and 'limit' parameters in a subsequent 'read_file' call. For example, to read the next section of the file, use offset: 200.\n\n--- FILE CONTENT (truncated) ---\n```\n\n### Streamlit Community Cloud Deployment\n\nRepoQuest is designed to deploy easily to Streamlit Community Cloud:\n\n1. Push this repository to GitHub (public repo)\n2. Go to [share.streamlit.io](https://share.streamlit.io)\n3. Deploy with these settings:\n  - **Repository:** Your GitHub repo\n  - **Branch:** main\n  - **Main file path:** `app/streamlit_app.py`\n4. No secrets or environment variables required\n5. The bundled demo repo works without file upload\n\nSee [infra/streamlit/README.md](infra/streamlit/README.md) for detailed deployment instructions.\n\n### Docker Development\n\nRun the deterministic app in Docker:\n\n```bash\ndocker compose up --build repoquest\n```\n\nRun the app with the optional async assistant service in mock mode:\n\n```bash\nREPOQUEST_AI_ENABLED=true \\\nREPOQUEST_ASSISTANT_SERVICE_URL=http://assistant:8765 \\\nREPOQUEST_ASSISTANT_SERVICE_PROVIDER=mock \\\ndocker compose --profile assistant up --build\n```\n\nFor a real model call, provide `CLAUDE_API_KEY` instead of the mock provider.\nDocker is optional and is not required for Streamlit Community Cloud.\n\n## How to Use\n\n### 1. Select Input Source\n\nChoose between:\n- **Demo Repo** - Bundled Mini Travel Planner (React + FastAPI)\n- **Upload ZIP** - Your own repository (max 25 MB)\n\n### 2. Generate Quest\n\nClick \"Generate Quest\" to analyze the repository. After analysis, the sidebar stays available with source status, upload/generate controls, app limits, and AI Review status so you can switch sources without losing orientation.\n\n### 3. Explore Results\n\nNavigate through tabs:\n- **Overview** - Project type, frameworks, entry points, next read, and architecture preview\n- **Architecture** - Conceptual system map plus technical dependency graph\n- **Files** - Repository tree and focused file/code analysis\n- **API Routes** - Detected endpoints linked to their route files\n- **Read** - Guided tour with one selected file preview at a time\n- **Components** - Important file cards with evidence and test ideas\n- **Work Plans** - Deterministic epics, tasks, and milestones\n- **Agent Workflows** - Step-by-step workflows ready for IBM Bob or another coding assistant\n- **Improve** - Test intelligence and onboarding quiz\n- **Export** - Download Markdown onboarding guide\n\nAbout/Built with IBM Bob content lives in the persistent sidebar.\n\n### 4. Export Guide\n\nGenerate and download a comprehensive Markdown onboarding document for your team.\n\n## Project Structure\n\n```\nrepoquest/\n|-- repoquest/     # Core analysis modules\n|  |-- scanner.py   # File scanning and ZIP handling\n|  |-- zip_safety.py  # ZIP security validation\n|  |-- indicator_rules.py # Rule definitions for deterministic analysis\n|  |-- detectors.py  # Framework and project detection\n|  |-- import_graph.py # Dependency graph generation\n|  |-- route_extractors.py # API route detection\n|  |-- architecture.py # Architecture map generation\n|  |-- reading_path.py # Reading path generation\n|  |-- response_templates.py # Deterministic wording and guidance\n|  |-- quest.py    # Component cards and quiz\n|  |-- workflows.py # Work plan and workflow generation\n|  |-- test_intelligence.py # Test impact and quality analysis\n|  |-- assistant_*.py # Optional manual AI assistant foundation\n|  |-- report.py    # Markdown report generation\n|  `-- models.py    # Data models\n|-- app/\n|  `-- streamlit_app.py # Streamlit UI\n|-- services/\n|  `-- assistant_service.py # Optional async assistant/model service\n|-- tests/       # Unit tests\n|-- examples/\n|  `-- demo_repos/   # Bundled demo repositories\n|-- infra/\n|  |-- local/     # Local development config\n|  |-- docker/    # Optional Dockerfiles for app and assistant service\n|  `-- streamlit/   # Streamlit Cloud config"
          }
        }
      }
    ],
    "status": "success",
    "timestamp": "2026-05-16T17:11:57.973Z",

    "description": "Reads and returns the content of a specified file. If the file is large, the content will be truncated. The tool's response will clearly indicate if truncation has occurred and will provide details on how to read more of the file using the 'offset' and 'limit' parameters. Handles text, images (PNG, JPG, GIF, WEBP, SVG, BMP), and PDF files. For text files, it can read specific line ranges.",
    "renderOutputAsMarkdown": true
  }
]

      "diff": "\n# Search: |||\nFor a real model call, provide `CLAUDE_API_KEY` instead of the mock provider.\nDocker is optional and is not required for Streamlit Community Cloud.\n\n## How to Use\n|||\n# Start Line: 130\n# Replace with: |||\nFor a real model call, provide `CLAUDE_API_KEY` instead of the mock provider.\nDocker is optional and is not required for Streamlit Community Cloud.\n\n## Deployment Profiles\n\nRepoQuest supports multiple deployment profiles to accommodate different use cases:\n\n| Profile | AI Enabled | Secrets Required | Network Calls | Best For |\n|---------|-----------|------------------|---------------|----------|\n| **Deterministic** | No | None | None | Public demo, Streamlit Cloud, default |\n| **Mock Assistant** | Yes | None | None | Testing, CI/CD, development |\n| **Cloud Assistant** | Yes | API key | Claude API | Live AI demo, development |\n| **Local Model** | Yes | None | Local only | Private/offline AI, experimentation |\n| **Service Assistant** | Yes | Varies | Varies | Async processing, scalability |\n\n### Deterministic Code Assistant (Default)\n\nThe default profile requires no secrets and makes no external API calls. This is the recommended profile for:\n- Public demos\n- Streamlit Community Cloud deployment\n- Offline development\n- CI/CD pipelines\n\n**Setup:**\n```bash\npython scripts/run_local.py\n# or\ndocker compose up --build repoquest\n```\n\n### Mock AI Assistant\n\nFor testing AI features without network calls or API costs:\n\n```bash\nREPOQUEST_AI_ENABLED=true \\\nREPOQUEST_ASSISTANT_PROVIDER=mock \\\npython scripts/run_local.py\n```\n\n### Cloud AI Assistant (Claude)\n\nFor live AI-assisted insights:\n\n```bash\nREPOQUEST_AI_ENABLED=true \\\nCLAUDE_API_KEY=your_key_here \\\npython scripts/run_local.py\n```\n\n**Streamlit Cloud:** Add secrets in dashboard:\n```toml\nREPOQUEST_AI_ENABLED = \"true\"\nCLAUDE_API_KEY = \"your_key_here\"\n```\n\n### Local Model Assistant\n\nFor private/offline AI using local model servers (Ollama, LM Studio, etc.):\n\n```bash\n# Start Ollama\nollama pull llama3.1\n\n# Run RepoQuest\nREPOQUEST_AI_ENABLED=true \\\nREPOQUEST_ASSISTANT_PROVIDER=local \\\nREPOQUEST_LOCAL_MODEL_BASE_URL=http://localhost:11434/v1 \\\nREPOQUEST_LOCAL_MODEL_NAME=llama3.1 \\\npython scripts/run_local.py\n```\n\n### Async Assistant Service\n\nFor scalable async processing with Docker:\n\n```bash\n# Mock mode\nREPOQUEST_AI_ENABLED=true \\\nREPOQUEST_ASSISTANT_SERVICE_URL=http://assistant:8765 \\\nREPOQUEST_ASSISTANT_SERVICE_PROVIDER=mock \\\ndocker compose --profile assistant up --build\n\n# Cloud mode\nREPOQUEST_AI_ENABLED=true \\\nREPOQUEST_ASSISTANT_SERVICE_URL=http://assistant:8765 \\\nCLAUDE_API_KEY=your_key_here \\\ndocker compose --profile assistant up --build\n\n# Local model mode\nREPOQUEST_AI_ENABLED=true \\\nREPOQUEST_ASSISTANT_SERVICE_URL=http://assistant:8765 \\\nREPOQUEST_ASSISTANT_SERVICE_PROVIDER=local \\\nREPOQUEST_LOCAL_MODEL_BASE_URL=http://host.docker.internal:11434/v1 \\\nREPOQUEST_LOCAL_MODEL_NAME=llama3.1 \\\ndocker compose --profile assistant up --build\n```\n\n**See [infra/docker/README.md](infra/docker/README.md) and [docs/assistant_mode.md](docs/assistant_mode.md) for complete deployment profile documentation.**\n\n## How to Use\n|||\n# Purpose: Add deployment profiles section to README\n"
